comment_text.sn12.52:1.2: Upādāniya is literally “graspable”. The simile evokes the secondary sense of upādāna as “fuel”, so I translate “(things that) fuel grasping”.

root_text.sn12.52:1.2: “Upādāniyesu, bhikkhave, dhammesu assādānupassino viharato taṇhā pavaḍḍhati.
root_text.sn12.52:2.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, dasannaṁ vā kaṭṭhavāhānaṁ vīsāya vā kaṭṭhavāhānaṁ tiṁsāya vā kaṭṭhavāhānaṁ cattārīsāya vā kaṭṭhavāhānaṁ mahāaggikkhandho jaleyya.
root_text.sn12.52:2.2: Tatra puriso kālena kālaṁ sukkhāni ceva tiṇāni pakkhipeyya, sukkhāni ca gomayāni pakkhipeyya, sukkhāni ca kaṭṭhāni pakkhipeyya.
root_text.sn12.52:2.3: Evañhi so, bhikkhave, mahāaggikkhandho tadāhāro tadupādāno ciraṁ dīghamaddhānaṁ jaleyya.
root_text.sn12.52:2.4: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, upādāniyesu dhammesu assādānupassino viharato taṇhā pavaḍḍhati.
root_text.sn12.52:3.1: Upādāniyesu, bhikkhave, dhammesu ādīnavānupassino viharato taṇhā nirujjhati.
root_text.sn12.52:4.1: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, dasannaṁ vā kaṭṭhavāhānaṁ vīsāya vā tiṁsāya vā cattārīsāya vā kaṭṭhavāhānaṁ mahāaggikkhandho jaleyya;
root_text.sn12.52:4.2: tatra puriso na kālena kālaṁ sukkhāni ceva tiṇāni pakkhipeyya, na sukkhāni ca gomayāni pakkhipeyya, na sukkhāni ca kaṭṭhāni pakkhipeyya.
root_text.sn12.52:4.3: Evañhi so, bhikkhave, mahāaggikkhandho purimassa ca upādānassa pariyādānā aññassa ca anupahārā anāhāro nibbāyeyya.
root_text.sn12.52:4.4: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, upādāniyesu dhammesu ādīnavānupassino viharato taṇhā nirujjhati,

translation_text.sn12.52:1.2: “There are things that fuel grasping. When you concentrate on the gratification provided by these things, your craving grows.
translation_text.sn12.52:1.3: Craving is a requirement for grasping.
translation_text.sn12.52:1.4: Grasping is a requirement for continued existence.
translation_text.sn12.52:1.5: Continued existence is a requirement for rebirth.
translation_text.sn12.52:1.6: Rebirth is a requirement for old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress to come to be.
translation_text.sn12.52:1.7: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.52:2.1: Suppose a great mass of fire was burning with ten, twenty, thirty, or forty loads of wood.
translation_text.sn12.52:2.2: And from time to time someone would toss in dry grass, cow dung, or wood.
translation_text.sn12.52:2.3: Fed and fuelled by that, the great mass of fire would burn for a long time.
translation_text.sn12.52:2.4: In the same way, there are things that fuel grasping. When you concentrate on the gratification provided by these things, your craving grows.
translation_text.sn12.52:2.5: Craving is a requirement for grasping. …
translation_text.sn12.52:2.6: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.sn12.52:3.1: There are things that fuel grasping. When you concentrate on the drawbacks of these things, your craving ceases.
translation_text.sn12.52:3.3: When grasping ceases, continued existence ceases.
translation_text.sn12.52:3.4: When continued existence ceases, rebirth ceases.
translation_text.sn12.52:3.5: When rebirth ceases, old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress cease.
translation_text.sn12.52:3.6: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.
translation_text.sn12.52:4.1: Suppose a great mass of fire was burning with ten, twenty, thirty, or forty loads of wood.
translation_text.sn12.52:4.2: And no-one would toss in dry grass, cow dung, or wood from time to time.
translation_text.sn12.52:4.3: As the original fuel is used up and no more is added, the great mass of fire would be extinguished due to not being fed.
translation_text.sn12.52:4.4: In the same way, there are things that fuel grasping. When you concentrate on the drawbacks of these things, your craving ceases.
translation_text.sn12.52:4.6: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.”
